payload.title: Treat Site lifecycle transformations as first-class Narada machinery
payload.summary: Operator and architect identified that Narada telos points toward governed Site lifecycle operations: absorb, split off, clone, fork, migrate, and re-instantiate Sites. This appears as important as task arc/chapter machinery because Sites are operational loci whose authority, provenance, trace, and re-instantiation semantics must remain coherent under transformation.
evidence: Current work created PC, User, Thoughts, Client Service, Data, and ELT Narada Sites or Site candidates, exposing repeated questions of where authority lives and how Site knowledge/machinery should travel.
Plural Embodiment, Singular Authority clarified that cloning or multiple embodiments must not imply duplicate mutation authority.
Inhabited Evolution clarified that Sites emerge from real operations and may later lift, split, or merge only when earned by pressure and evidence.
Constructive Universalization by Re-instantiation implies Site templates and absorbed machinery should prove they can re-host originating cases.
proposal: Create a Narada product/design arc or equivalent governed effort for Site lifecycle transformations: clone, fork, split, absorb, migrate, re-instantiate, and archive.

Define Site lifecycle operations as governed transformations over loci, authority maps, provenance, config, evidence, inbox/task state, templates, and derived projections, not raw folder copy/move operations.
Each transformation should declare whether it preserves, transfers, duplicates, or refuses mutation authority for each mutation class.
Site clone should support embodiment/read-only/forwarding/authority-bearing modes under explicit policy.
Site split should extract a sub-locus with traceable provenance and explicit authority migration or residual linkage.
Site absorb should merge local/sidecar Site knowledge or machinery into a broader Site or Narada proper only through governed admission and re-instantiation evidence.
Site re-instantiation should rebuild a Site from template + durable trace + config + evidence and verify that the originating case still runs through the lifted form.
Treat this as potentially comparable in importance to task lifecycle machinery because it governs how Narada operational loci themselves evolve.
payload.recommendation: Promote to important Narada architecture/product arc for first-class Site lifecycle machinery, with doctrine, command surface, config schema, doctor/preflight checks, and re-instantiation proofs.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
